description_long: This bungalow features a multi-level design with a prominent cross-gabled roof and beige siding, set on a rectangular green lawn. The front facade includes a small, covered entry porch with large windows, providing a welcoming appearance. The structure has a combination of single and double-story sections, with the taller portion at the rear and a lower, extended wing at the front. Windows are arranged asymmetrically, with several grouped on the sides and a large window facing the front. The roof is finished in brown shingles, and the foundation is slightly raised above the ground. The overall style is reminiscent of Craftsman bungalows, with practical, homey details and a focus on horizontal lines.
description: A beige, cross-gabled bungalow with a raised foundation, brown shingle roof, and asymmetrical window arrangement, featuring a small covered porch and multi-level design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the entry porch, large front windows, and the main cross-gabled roof structure.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the extended wing, grouped side windows, and the transition between single and double-story sections.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the taller section of the bungalow and minimal window openings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the rear wing, a large side window, and the connection between the two main roof sections.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with grouped side windows and large front window
